players.name: Alireza Beiranvand
players.bio: Iran's long-serving No.1, Beiranvand grew up in poverty, sleeping rough before football changed his life. His thunderous punching and brave shot-stopping made him a fans' hero, and his 86 caps speak volumes.
players.facts: Once homeless as a teenager, Beiranvand walked hours to training sessions in borrowed boots. Football literally saved his life.

players.name: Saleh Hardani
players.bio: Hardani is Esteghlal's energetic right-back, a marauding presence who loves to bomb forward. With 18 caps and a goal to his name, the Khuzestan-born defender is still writing his international story.
players.facts: Hardani hails from Bahmaei — a town so small, his international debut probably made every wall in it.

players.name: Ehsan Hajsafi
players.bio: A genuine Iranian legend with 146 caps, Hajsafi is Team Melli's heartbeat and captain. His longevity, leadership, and overlapping runs have defined Iran's left flank for over a decade.
players.facts: Hajsafi made headlines at Qatar 2022 by openly expressing sympathy for protesters — a rare and brave moment of public dissent.

players.name: Shojae Khalilzadeh
players.bio: A tough, experienced centre-back who has been a rock at the heart of Iran's defence. At 37, Khalilzadeh brings the kind of no-nonsense defending that only years of hard knocks can teach.
players.facts: Born in Bahnemir, a village in Mazandaran, Khalilzadeh has been keeping strikers quiet since before some teammates finished school.

players.name: Milad Mohammadi
players.full_name: Milad Mohammadi Keshmarzi; میلاد محمدی کشمرزی
players.bio: A reliable and versatile left-back with 76 caps, Mohammadi has graced European football and returned to Persepolis. His crossing ability and defensive discipline make him a consistent asset for Iran.
players.facts: Mohammadi played in the Dutch Eredivisie with AZ Alkmaar — one of the few Team Melli players with tulip-country pedigree.

players.name: Saeid Ezatolahi
players.full_name: Saeid Ezatolahi Afagh; سعید عزت‌اللهی آفاق
players.bio: A disciplined engine in Iran's midfield, Ezatolahi screens the back four with tireless energy and reads the game well beyond his years. His time in the UAE with Shabab Al Ahli keeps him sharp.
players.facts: Ezatolahi was linked with Rostov as a teenager — he's been on Europe's radar since he was barely old enough to drive.

players.name: Alireza Jahanbakhsh
players.bio: Jahanbakhsh is Iran's most celebrated winger of his generation, with stints at AZ, Brighton, and Feyenoord. Tricky, fast, and capable of the spectacular, his 17 international goals underline his class.
players.facts: He scored a stunning bicycle kick for Feyenoord in the Europa League — one of the best goals you'll ever see from an Iranian player.

players.name: Mohammad Mohebi
players.bio: Mohebi is one of Iran's most exciting young talents, a left winger with 14 goals in just 36 caps — a remarkable return. His time in Russia with Rostov has sharpened his game considerably.
players.facts: Mohebi and Mehdi Ghayedi are both born in Bushehr in December 1998 — Bushehr's festive season clearly had something in the water.

players.name: Mehdi Taremi
players.bio: Iran's all-time great striker, Taremi has scored 60 goals in 105 caps — a staggering record. Composed, clever, and lethal in the air, he was the star of Qatar 2022 and remains Team Melli's focal point.
players.facts: Taremi scored a penalty in the 100th minute vs Portugal at Qatar 2022 with Iran already eliminated — pure class under zero pressure.

players.name: Mehdi Ghayedi
players.bio: A creative, box-to-box talent with an eye for goal, Ghayedi has 10 goals in 30 caps — a fine return for an attacking midfielder. His move to Al Nasr shows the Gulf's appetite for Iran's rising stars.
players.facts: Three of Iran's squad — Taremi, Mohebi, and Ghayedi — all hail from Bushehr. The port city is basically a football factory.

players.name: Ali Alipour
players.bio: Alipour is a physical, hard-working centre-forward who leads the line with determination. Still developing his international pedigree at 30, this World Cup could be his moment to shine for Team Melli.
players.facts: Born on 11/11 — Remembrance Day and, for Iranian football fans, hopefully a day worth remembering too.

players.name: Payam Niazmand
players.bio: Niazmand is the reliable understudy between the posts for Iran, serving as Persepolis's No.1 domestically. With 15 caps, he brings solid competition to the goalkeeping ranks.
players.facts: As backup keeper at a World Cup, Niazmand may touch the ball less than the ball boys — but he'll be ready if called upon.

players.name: Hossein Kanaanizadegan
players.bio: Kanaanizadegan is a versatile and experienced Iranian defender with 65 caps and an impressive 6 international goals — exceptional for a defender. A Persepolis stalwart and team leader.
players.facts: Six international goals from defence is the kind of stat that makes centre-forwards question their career choices.

players.name: Saman Ghoddos
players.bio: Born in Malmö to an Iranian father, Ghoddos chose Iran over Sweden and hasn't looked back. A creative, technically gifted midfielder who earned a move to Brentford, he brings a European DNA to Team Melli.
players.facts: Ghoddos is the rare World Cup player who could theoretically have faced his current teammates while playing for Sweden's youth sides.

players.name: Rouzbeh Cheshmi
players.bio: Cheshmi is a combative defensive midfielder who delivers when it matters most. Known for his physicality and reading of the game, his 40 caps reflect a solid if understated international career.
players.facts: Cheshmi scored a late, crucial goal vs Wales at Qatar 2022 to help fire Iran into the knockout stages. Big moment, cool head.

players.name: Mehdi Torabi
players.bio: Torabi is a tricky, pacey left winger who has been a fan favourite throughout his career. His 7 goals from wide positions in 52 caps show he's more than just a speedster — he can finish too.
players.facts: Torabi comes from Eshtehard, a town better known for its industrial park than its football academies. He changed that narrative.

players.name: Aria Yousefi
players.bio: At just 24, Yousefi is one of the youngest members of the squad and a bright future for Iran's right flank. The Sepahan right-back is quick, progressive, and already has a goal to his name in 14 caps.
players.facts: Born in 2002, Yousefi was just 12 when Iran last appeared at a World Cup in Brazil 2014. Now he's playing in one.

players.name: Amirhossein Hosseinzadeh
players.bio: Hosseinzadeh is a dynamic young winger with an impressive 5 goals in just 18 caps. At 25, the Tehran-born Tractor flyer is one of the most exciting attacking prospects in the Iranian squad.
players.facts: Five international goals from 18 caps at age 25 — Hosseinzadeh's goals-per-game ratio would make many veteran forwards blush.

players.name: Ali Nemati
players.bio: A composed and dependable centre-back, Nemati brings solidity to Iran's defensive options. The Foolad stopper has steadily accumulated 17 caps and offers reliable cover in the heart of defence.
players.facts: Nemati hails from Nishapur — the ancient city also birthplace of poet Omar Khayyám. Defending poetry, you might say.

players.name: Shahriyar Moghanlou
players.bio: Moghanlou is a forward who has carved out a steady international career with 21 caps and 2 goals. Now plying his trade in the UAE with Kalba, he offers experience and versatility to Iran's attack.
players.facts: Moghanlou and squadmate Ghoddos both play for Kalba in the UAE — making for a very Iranian corner of the Emirates.

players.name: Mohammad Ghorbani
players.bio: One of Iran's youngest players at 24, Ghorbani is a disciplined defensive midfielder already operating in the UAE with Al Wahda. His 16 caps suggest a bright future as a midfield anchor.
players.facts: Born in Arak in 2001, Ghorbani is part of a generation that grew up watching Taremi score goals. Now he plays alongside him.

players.name: Hossein Hosseini
players.full_name: Seyed Majid Hosseini; سید مجید حسینی
players.bio: A versatile, experienced defender who provides quality cover across the backline, Hosseini has 13 caps for Iran with Sepahan. At 33, he brings calm authority and dressing-room experience.
players.facts: Sharing a name with arguably Iran's most-celebrated historical figure means Hossein Hosseini carries a weighty label with ease.

players.name: Ramin Rezaeian
players.full_name: Ramin Rezaeian Semeskandi; رامین رضائیان سمسکندی
players.bio: A veteran right-back with 74 caps and an astonishing 8 international goals, Rezaeian is one of the most attack-minded defenders Iran has ever produced. This is likely his farewell World Cup.
players.facts: Eight goals from right-back in 74 caps. Rezaeian scores more than some strikers. Someone should check he's playing the right position.

players.name: Dennis Eckert
players.bio: Born in Bonn, Germany, Eckert qualifies through Iranian heritage and could make his debut on the grandest stage of all at 2026. A powerfully built centre-forward with Belgian club experience at Standard Liège.
players.facts: Eckert would be uncapped going into the World Cup — potentially making his international debut at the actual World Cup. Baptism of fire.

players.name: Danial Eiri
players.bio: A 22-year-old centre-back from Golestan province, Eiri is one of the most exciting defensive prospects in Iranian football. Uncapped heading into 2026, this World Cup squad place is a huge vote of confidence.
players.facts: Eiri was born in 2003 — making him young enough that his first World Cup memory might be Germany vs Brazil 7-1 as a toddler.

players.name: Amirmohammad Razzaghinia
players.bio: At just 20, Razzaghinia is the baby of the squad and one of the most talented young midfielders in Iranian football. Four caps already hint at huge potential for this Esteghlal starlet.
players.facts: Born in April 2006, Razzaghinia was barely a teenager when Iran played at Qatar 2022. Now he's in the squad himself. Remarkable.
